description_long: This object consists of a cluster of industrial control cabinets, typically used for electrical distribution or power management in urban environments. The cabinets are metallic, rectangular, and show significant wear, with visible graffiti, scratches, and dirt. Multiple thick cables, both red and black, emerge from the cabinets and connect to various points, indicating active electrical use. The cabinets feature several access doors, some with visible latches or handles, and a few smaller control boxes mounted above or beside the main units. The surrounding area is tiled, and the cabinets are mounted against a concrete wall, with some exposed wiring and damaged surfaces. There are no prominent warning labels, but the utilitarian design and cable arrangement suggest a high-voltage or critical infrastructure function.
description: A group of weathered metal control cabinets with exposed cables and access doors, mounted against a concrete wall. The cabinets are covered in graffiti and dirt, with multiple thick red and black wires emerging from them.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main faces of the cabinets, access doors, graffiti, and the majority of exposed cables.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the cabinets and the way cables are routed along the side.
description_view_2: Rear or back view showing the wall mounting, damaged surfaces, and fewer visible cables.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, highlighting the thickness of the cabinets and the cable entry points at the base.
